title: env-schema.1 — per-repo typed environment schema export
description: Machine export of a repo's env.ts (env-catalog initiative, item 1). Semantic rules that JSON Schema cannot express — duplicate variable names, sort order by name, default_state membership in flag_states, flag default == default_state — are enforced by scripts/src/contracts/env-schema.ts (validateEnvSchemaDocument). The human contract is docs/architecture/contracts/env-schema.md.

repo.description: Canonical repo id from control-center repos.yaml, e.g. "trading-platform".

generated_from.description: Repo-relative path of the env.ts module the document was exported from.

variables.description: Sorted ascending by name; names unique (enforced by the TS validator).

required.description: true = the app refuses to start without it (fail-fast); implies default == null.

default.description: String form exactly as it would appear in a .env file; null = no default. Always null for secret and required variables.

secret.description: true = the value never appears in the schema, ENV.md or any .env*.example — only the name and shape.

flag.description: true = deploy-time feature flag; requires flag_states and default_state.

flag_states.description: Subset of the ecosystem flag vocabulary this flag actually supports.

default_state.description: Must be a member of flag_states (enforced by the TS validator).

owner_unit.description: Systemd unit / logical service that owns the variable, e.g. "trading-platform.service".

consumers.description: Repo-relative modules that read the variable (importers of env.ts).
